# Product Requirements Document

<!-- PRD generated by gastown. Fill in each section below. -->

## Product Overview

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Describe what this product is and what problem it solves. One paragraph.

## Target Users

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Who uses this? Describe the primary persona and their context.

## Core Features

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> List features as bullets. Each feature: name, one-line description, priority (must-have / nice-to-have).

## User Stories

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Write at least 3 user stories in the format: As a [user], I want [action], so that [benefit].

## Tech Stack

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Language, framework, database, external services, hosting environment.

## Data Model

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Entities, relationships, key fields. Can be a table or bullet list.

## API / Interface Design

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Endpoints (for APIs), screens (for UIs), CLI commands (for tools). Include input/output formats.

## Non-Functional Requirements

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Performance, security, scalability, accessibility constraints.

## Acceptance Criteria

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Specific, measurable conditions for "done". Numbered list.

## Out of Scope

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> What NOT to build. Prevents scope creep.

## Authentication & Authorization

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Auth method, roles, permissions. Skip if no auth needed.

## Error Handling

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Error format, retry strategy, logging approach.

## Testing Strategy

<!-- STATUS: empty -->

> Test framework, coverage target, key test scenarios.
